name: alexfordlabs
description: Public Claude Code plugin marketplace by Alex Ford Labs. A growing collection of design-first developer tooling: project-architect bootstraps any project type with research-augmented questioning, ADR-tracked decisions, parallel doc generation, and project-local Claude Code configuration; its companion reverse-engineer recovers a design from a foreign/brownfield project and hands it back to project-architect through a shared state contract.

plugins.name: project-architect
plugins.description: Project architecture orchestrator running every subagent on the latest Opus Claude model. Interviews you across 12 phases, deciding the architecture before the tech stack (domain shape and boundaries first, infrastructure second), and dispatches 7 specialized subagents (architecture, research, document / CLAUDE.md / tooling authoring, iteration, and design-recovery). Files a MADR-4 ADR per decision the moment it's made — real timestamps, never back-filled — then gates the result with a 36-check, 4-tier mechanical auditor before LOCK — including enforcement that every generated config rides research-resolved newest-stable version pins (audit check version_pins_recorded; numeric pins honored), never a stale fallback. State is event-sourced through a single architect-brain binary: an append-only event log plus replayable per-concern projections at schema 4.0, where replay(events) == projections is an enforced (FATAL) invariant — so every run is resumable, tamper-evident, and never hand-edited. Document selection is a declarative 107-document catalog (conditions-DSL + topological ordering → parallel document-author dispatch); 9 Golden Paths pre-fill common stacks (modern SaaS, AI/RAG, mobile cross-platform, high-performance API, Rust CLI, MCP server, agentic system, Claude Code plugin, language interpreter); and a read-only Preflight situation-router resumes, migrates, upgrades (/upgrade-project), or re-architects (/re-architect) a prior project — or starts fresh, optionally seeded from a reverse-engineer recovered design. Generates design docs + a root and per-folder CLAUDE.md router + a stack-aware .claude/ tooling tree + 3 always-on plan docs (CLAUDE.md, tooling, and next-step) + /scaffold, /implement, and /iterate-design router commands for the project it bootstraps. Supports 19+ project types — web, mobile, multi-platform, API, CLI, library, desktop, browser extension, games, AI/ML, data pipelines, embedded/IoT, infrastructure, Claude Code plugins, MCP servers, agentic systems, Web3, scientific code, AR/VR, and programming-language design (7 dedicated templates: grammar, semantics, type system, stdlib, toolchain, bootstrap plan, stability/RFC).

plugins.name: reverse-engineer
plugins.description: Brownfield design-recovery orchestrator — the companion to project-architect, pointed the other way. Take a foreign project (arbitrary code, docs, notes, half-finished folder trees — anything with no project-architect history) and reverse-engineer its design: detect what kind of artifact it is → understand it (file/dependency inventory, dependency map, live current-version research on every framework it actually uses, and inferred requirements read back out of the code) → recover the design into a reviewable RECOVERED_DESIGN.md → triage every recovered decision (keep / revise / drop, low-confidence-first, human-validated) → emit a flat, re-derivable decisions keyspace + a schema-3.1 origin:"reverse-engineered" state → hand off to project-architect via the shared state contract (its output rides project-architect's seeded-greenfield / import-decisions path, so you continue bootstrapping with the recovered design already in hand). The differentiator is never-stale grounding: it resolves the newest-stable version of each detected dependency live at recovery time rather than recalling from training. Bidirectionally interoperable with project-architect.
